topic: Honcho memory patterns vs cnogo - what to steal

sources.description: Honcho source code: FastAPI backend, deriver pipeline, dialectic agent, vector store abstractions, SDK
cnogo memory engine: 20+ modules, ~6600 lines, SQLite + JSONL sync
cnogo workflow policy and memory configuration

summary: Honcho's core innovation is derived memory — continuous background reasoning extracts observations from conversations at four levels (explicit, deductive, inductive, contradiction)
cnogo's memory engine is a deterministic task coordination layer that only stores explicitly created state; it has no observation extraction, semantic search, or contradiction detection

Six transferable patterns identified: contradiction detection (P0), observation extraction (P1), multi-perspective notes (P1), conversation context capture (P2), background insight derivation (P2), semantic task search (P3)
Honcho's vector store and LLM-in-the-loop patterns are NOT adoptable due to cnogo's stdlib-only and offline-first constraints
The foundational insight: memory should be derived, not just stored — adding observation extraction would enable multiple downstream improvements
recommendation: Adopt contradiction detection immediately (pure validation logic, no new deps). Then build observation extraction as the foundation for multi-perspective notes, context capture, and background insight derivation. Route to /shape memory-intelligence for initiative-level architecture decisions.
